CIPHER.302.cipherString: Ultimately, we hope to find a fundamental theory that explains everything with no input parameters.
CIPHER.302.question: While scrolling through an old floppy disk labeled "TOP SECRET", you discovered this quote by Nathan Seiberg hidden as an Aristocrat. What does it say?
CIPHER.302.author: Nathan Seiberg

CIPHER.288.cipherString: You are judged many times more by what you do in groups than for what you do as individuals.
CIPHER.288.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote by Compton Gage encoded using a Checkerboard with a polybius key of COMPLAINER. What does it say?
CIPHER.288.author: Compton Gage

CIPHER.298.cipherString: The problem inherent in the surface of things, and only in the surface of things, is the heart of things.
CIPHER.298.question: Found inside a hollowed-out periodic table book: this Aristocrat Cipher hides Leo Strauss's insight. What does it say?
CIPHER.298.author: Leo Strauss

CIPHER.283.cipherString: This day says nothing different to me than to you.
CIPHER.283.question: You came across the following written on a wall in a cave. You are told that it's a quote by Neamat Imam encoded as a Baconian. What does it say?
CIPHER.283.author: Neamat Imam

CIPHER.286.cipherString: Entrenamos la mente para que podamos disfrutar de una mayor paz, felicidad, sabiduría y ecuanimidad.
CIPHER.286.question: You open a forgotten email draft saved in the school's computer system. It holds a quote by Allan Lokos in Spanish encoded in a Xenocrypt using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.286.author: Allan Lokos
CIPHER.286.translation: We train the mind so that we can enjoy greater peace, happiness, wisdom & equanimity.

CIPHER.295.cipherString: Life must not be a novel that is given to us, but one that is made by us.
CIPHER.295.question: You find this encoded quote written inside the cover of a chemistry reference book. It's attributed to Novalis, encoded as a Complete Columnar. The quote has NOVEL somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.295.author: Novalis

CIPHER.282.cipherString: Music, when healthy, is the teacher of perfect order; and also when depraved, the teacher of perfect disorder.
CIPHER.282.question: The marching band's sheet music rearranged itself into this Aristocrat pattern—it's a quote by Ruskin encoded using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word when twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.282.author: Ruskin

CIPHER.287.cipherString: He that speaks the truth will find himself in sufficiently dramatic situations.
CIPHER.287.question: During a field trip, you notice these strange markings on a metal plaque. They represent a quote by Prof. Wilson, encoded using a Complete Columnar Cipher. The quote has TRUTH somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.287.author: Prof. Wilson

CIPHER.277.cipherString: a person who tries to use reasoning to explain faith gets lost in the wilderness of incomprehension.
CIPHER.277.question: You found the following carved into the bark of a hollow log. You are told that it's a quote by Janvier Chando encoded as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word TO twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.277.author: Janvier Chando

CIPHER.281.cipherString: I've put something aside for a rainy day. It's an umbrella.
CIPHER.281.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote, encrypted using a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of BOTHER and polybius key of SHOULDERING. What does it say?
CIPHER.281.author: Unknown

CIPHER.296.cipherString: See? That's what happens when you keep people from doing what they do best: It makes them insane.
CIPHER.296.question: Hidden in the margin of a library book on black holes: decode this Aristocrat version of Rene Balcer using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.296.author: Rene Balcer

CIPHER.297.cipherString: The thermometer of success is merely the jealousy of the malcontents.
CIPHER.297.question: Someone left this coded message in the school lab. You are told it's a quote by Salvador Dali encoded with a Checkerboard with a polybius key of UNSCRAMBLED. What does it say?
CIPHER.297.author: Salvador Dali

CIPHER.280.cipherString: Begin every New Year a year ahead. End every year three months ahead. Watch your goals be achieved.
CIPHER.280.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Richie Norton using the Aristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.280.author: Richie Norton

CIPHER.299.cipherString: All of science is nothing more than the refinement of everyday thinking.
CIPHER.299.question: A glitchy hologram in the school planetarium displays this Porta version of Albert Einstein's words encoded using a keyword of SWAYED. What does it say?
CIPHER.299.author: Albert Einstein

CIPHER.290.cipherString: All that stuff about mainstream social media is interesting enough, which is to say not at all interesting.
CIPHER.290.question: A coded message is discovered inside a fortune cookie. It turns out to be a social media quote, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.290.author: Social media

CIPHER.278.cipherString: To conquer fear is the beginning of wisdom.
CIPHER.278.question: Bertrand Russell offers an observation that has been encoded as a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with TOCO. What does it say?
CIPHER.278.author: Bertrand Russell

CIPHER.293.cipherString: The superior man is modest in his speech, but exceeds in his actions.
CIPHER.293.question: A message flashes briefly on a malfunctioning computer screen. It turns out to be a quote by Confucius encoded using a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of SANITY and polybius key of HYPNOTIZED. What does it say?
CIPHER.293.author: Confucius

CIPHER.292.cipherString: Society is itself a kind of organism, an enormously powerful one, but unfortunately not a very wise one.
CIPHER.292.question: You find a coded sequence scratched into the surface of a lab stool. It turns out to be a quote by Isaac Asimov, written in an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word one twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.292.author: Isaac Asimov

CIPHER.291.question: You uncover this encoded note tucked behind a microscope slide. It's a Cryptarithm. What do the values 569371 0134 7281 decode to?

CIPHER.285.cipherString: A doctor can bury his mistakes but an architect can only advise his clients to plant vines.
CIPHER.285.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Frank Lloyd Wright, encrypted as a Complete Columnar. The quote has STAKES somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.285.author: Frank Lloyd Wright

CIPHER.276.question: Your school-issued Chromebook's login screen hijacked itself with this Hill encoded quote from Heroides using a keyword of REND. What does it say?
CIPHER.276.author: Heroides

CIPHER.301.cipherString: Every statesman must accept the choice of the people.
CIPHER.301.question: A note falls out of a borrowed library book. The message is a quote by Petro Poroshenko, written as a Fractionated Morse Cipher. You are told that the quote starts with EVERY. What does it say?
CIPHER.301.author: Petro Poroshenko

CIPHER.289.cipherString: Do you want to tell the truth, or do you want to tell a story?
CIPHER.289.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Robert Charles Wilson, written in a Baconian Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.289.author: Robert Charles Wilson

CIPHER.284.cipherString: His purposes will ripen fast, Unfolding every hour; The bud may have a bitter taste, But sweet will be the flower.
CIPHER.284.question: A mysterious postcard arrives from an unknown sender. It contains a coded quote by William Cowper, written in an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word will twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.284.author: William Cowper

CIPHER.279.question: Your smart fridge started flashing this cryptic message at 3 a.m. It's encoded in a Cryptarithm. What do the values 56773 2869 1046 decode to?

CIPHER.294.cipherString: If you want to correct your own failings, you do not have the time to waste in blaming other people.
CIPHER.294.question: This coded note is discovered inside a sealed envelope labeled “Confidential.” It's a quote from A Calendar of Wisdom, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.294.author: A Calendar of Wisdom

CIPHER.300.cipherString: I don't know of any sport where the goalposts can shift the way they do with endurance swimming.
CIPHER.300.question: The gym's scoreboard glitched during dodgeball to show this Aristocrat Cipher by Lewis Pugh using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.300.author: Lewis Pugh
